creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
(In reply to Tobias Kortkamp from comment #13)

FYI:

I tried the small test sequence using releng/13 (-p3) chroot instead
of 12.2 and did not get a crash.

I guess that the implication is the the bootstrap involved for rust
was built on 12 and used as a pre-built binary on 14? I've not had
any variant of 12 around for a rather long time. So anything tied
to 12 in building rust would have to have shown up from outside my
system.


For reference:

# file bt
bt: ELF 64-bit LSB executable, ARM aarch64, version 1 (FreeBSD), dynamically linked, interpreter /libexec/ld-elf.so.1, for FreeBSD 13.0 (1300139), FreeBSD-style, with debug_info, not stripped

# uname -apKU
FreeBSD CA72_16Gp_ZFS 14.0-CURRENT FreeBSD 14.0-CURRENT #7 main-n247651-a00d703f2f43-dirty: Wed Jun 30 15:11:11 PDT 2021     root@CA72_16Gp_ZFS:/usr/obj/BUILDs/main-CA72-nodbg-clang/usr/main-src/arm64.aarch64/sys/GENERIC-NODBG-CA72  arm64 aarch64 1400024 1400024

# ./bt
#
